senses.definition: In a manner related to or resembling a presbytery or presbyters (elders) in church government.
examples: The council discussed the issue presbyterially, with input from several elders.
They organized the meeting presbyterially, following the structure of the elder-led system.

senses.definition: Plural form referring to matters or items pertaining to presbyteries or presbyterial governance.
examples: The documents outline several presbyterials that govern regional churches.
They collected presbyterials from across the network.

senses.definition: Relating to or characteristic of a Presbyterian church or its governance; Presbyterian in denomination.
examples: She attends a Presbyterian church.
The Presbyterian system uses elected elders to govern the church.

senses.definition: To make something governed or organized in accordance with Presbyterian principles or structures.
examples: The new constitution was presbyterianised to align with elder-led governance.
They attempted to presbyterianise the organizational framework.

senses.definition: The Protestant Christian church government and doctrine that uses a system of elected elders (presbyters) and presbyteries.
examples: Presbyterianism emphasizes rule by eldership and doctrinal Calvinist roots.
They studied Presbyterianism to understand its church governance.

senses.definition: To adopt or implement Presbyterian form of governance or doctrine; to make something Presbyterian in character.
examples: The organization was Presbyterianized to include elder oversight.
They decided to Presbyterianize the governance model.

senses.definition: In a manner typical of Presbyterian governance or doctrine; in a way that reflects Presbyterian principles.
examples: The council acted presbyterianly, with careful elder input.
They organized the meeting presbyterianly, following established procedures.

senses.definition: Members or followers of the Presbyterian church.
examples: The Presbyterians gathered for worship.
Presbyterians emphasize elder-led governance.

senses.definition: Groups of elders and ministers that govern local congregations in Presbyterian church structure; the regional governing bodies.
examples: The presbyteries meet to decide on church policy.
Membership in several presbyteries affects presidency decisions.

senses.definition: The body of presbyters or the spiritual leadership within a diocese or church.
examples: The presbyterium congregated to discuss the new policy.
He spoke with the presbyterium about the church's future.

senses.definition: Elders in the Christian church who are part of the governance; priests in some traditions.
examples: The presbyters led the council meeting.
She consulted the presbyters before making a decision.

senses.definition: The office or tenure of a presbyter; the body of presbyters collectively.
examples: Her presbytership lasted several decades.
The presbytership provided governance for the denomination.

senses.definition: Multiple offices or tenures of presbyters; collective elder leadership positions.
examples: The presbyterships across regions were reorganized.
Several presbyterships met to review church policy.

senses.definition: A district governed by a group of presbyters; also the house of a Catholic priest in some contexts.
examples: The presbytery met to discuss church matters.
They visited the priest at the presbytery.

senses.definition: Plural form; not a standard common word; may refer to items or records associated with presbyteries in specialized contexts.
examples: They filed the presbytes for the regional church audit.

senses.definition: A plural or genus-related term in ecclesiastical contexts; not a common everyday word in English.
examples: The term presbytia appeared in historical church documents.

senses.definition: Of or relating to presbytery or Presbyterian governance; archaic or rare in modern usage.
examples: A presbytic tradition influenced their liturgical practices.

senses.definition: Taxonomic or scholarly plural term; not common in everyday usage; may refer to a subfamily within a biological naming system.
examples: The fossil group belongs to the Presbytinae subfamily in paleontology texts.

senses.definition: A genus in biology; not common in everyday language.
examples: Presbytis is a genus of old world monkeys.

senses.definition: A doctrine or practice associated with presbyterian governance; rarely used in modern English.
examples: The treatise discusses presbytism in early church governance.

senses.definition: Plural of presbytism; rare in ordinary use.
examples: Various presbytisms are discussed in the historical text.

senses.definition: To scan or examine something in advance, especially before a main process or action.
examples: They will prescan the data before the full analysis.
The system can prescan files for viruses.

senses.definition: Anatomical term referring to the area in front of the scapula (shoulder blade); used in medical contexts.
examples: Pain under the prescapula can indicate muscle strain.

senses.definition: Situated in front of the scapula; relating to the prescapular area.
examples: Prescapular lymph nodes were examined during the checkup.

senses.definition: Latin-derived taxonomic or anatomical term; rarely used in general English.
examples: The paper cites the prescapularis muscle in its schematic diagram.
